danger_signals.visual_form: Enlarged version of monster after first battle segment
danger_signals.meaning: Escalated danger; second-phase boss encounter with increased difficulty

danger_signals.visual_form: Opponent stance and proximity on fighting screen
danger_signals.meaning: Readiness to attack; spatial threat indicator

safety_signals.meaning: Player power amplification; transition to overwhelming force state
Persistent player survivability; quantified safety margin

faction_indicators.color_identity: Red (Jason), Blue, Pink, Yellow, Black primary ranger colors; Green (unlockable)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Ranger morphed form; distinctive costume per character
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Player-controlled fighter in arena; heroic stance animation

faction_indicators.color_identity: Distinct from ranger palette; monstrous design
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Monster creature design; putty patrol uniform
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Regular-sized and giant variants of same monster
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: CPU-controlled opponent spawn; arena occupancy indicates active threat

faction_indicators.color_identity: Dragon/mechanical silhouette contrast to ranger forms
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Unlockable after Green Ranger defeat; oversized battlefield presence

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Five original ranger character models available in roster
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Standard fighting movesets; no mecha power available

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Green Ranger defeated in story progression
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Green Ranger character model and Dragonzord mecha unlock in UI
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Expanded character roster; new moveset and mecha options in gameplay

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Progressive opponent difficulty increase reflected in monster scaling
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: CPU opponents display more aggressive combat patterns

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Transition from ranger to mecha combat
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Screen shift from standard-sized arena to giant-sized battle arena
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Combat mechanics shift to accommodate larger entity scale

environmental_storytelling.example: Initial selection screen shows five original rangers; visual diversity through distinct color schemes and costume designs communicates team structure
Green Ranger hidden until defeated in scenario mode; visual absence → presence communicates narrative progression and increasing threat integration
Monster grows to giant size in second battle segment; visual escalation mirrors power-scaling narrative of show mythology
Dragonzord and Megazord fight segments visualize the show's escalation from ranger-level to mecha-level threats

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Attacks player ranger; demonstrates AI aggression patterns
Enhanced attack frequency and damage output; increased arena control
Behavior complexity scales with round progression; later opponents use more special moves
Serves as Scenario mode opponent; visual unavailability in selection until defeated
Controllable by player after ranger defeat segments; visually dominates arena through scale

notes: Genesis version employs two-phase battle structure (regular → giant monster) to communicate escalating threat. Faction identity relies primarily on character color differentiation and morphed ranger silhouettes vs. monster design contrast. World state progression is primarily conveyed through roster unlock mechanics rather than environmental change. The game lacks dynamic environmental world-state shifts; state changes manifest through opponent substitution and difficulty scaling only.
